title: feeling depressed
wikitext:
[]: feeling depressed Post by bladerj » Sat Dec 20, 2014 6:18 pm its like waiting for your death, you dont know when it will come, but you know its soon and then you just have to make peace wth the fact and acept the inevitable...... The lack of any news for a full year, the goodbye to the franchise,.......i can't take it.....

[]: Post by lastcody » Sat Dec 20, 2014 6:26 pm uhhh did you hear the news http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/17010/Until-You-Come-to-me-EVA-SHORT-FILM-40-HYPE/

[]: Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Dec 20, 2014 6:27 pm It happens, I remember watching EoE and was all like "This is it? This all there is or ever will be? Why did he run out of plot and money? Was his plot not good enough for money? Why won't he do anything?" Then I was like "Chaddy, you got to chill bro, he'll get smarter and craftier, he'll grow into the creator you want to him to be, he may not be the creator you need but he'll be the creator you deserve."

[]: Post by Jimbob » Sat Dec 20, 2014 8:57 pm It's just fictional entertainment. There's always more somewhere.

[]: Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Dec 20, 2014 9:37 pm Please don't lock this thread. I get depressed too sometimes about the franchise.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 20, 2014 10:09 pm (I'd lock this thread if I still had the power to.) bladerj : Assuming there is not some kind of pathological depression causing you to feel depressed about things that logically do not warrant it -- for which professional help is recommended -- I would suggest investing yourself in hobbies that do not involve Eva . If you don't have a job, get one, even if it's a shitty one you think you're too good for. If you're just trolling, I would suggest investing yourself in hobbies other than trolling.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 20, 2014 10:13 pm bladerj: Like LastCody posted, there's no need to be depressed with the Until You Come to Me and Peaceful Times videos. I linked to this on that news post the other day, but again, for reference: 1731298478 wrote: Every time Anno works on Eva he breaks down. The original Eva nearly killed him. The new project (http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3369.html) is by no means a diversion but something he has to do leading up to the next Eva film. He broke down again after he finished Q, but being married helped him to bear it. He was told by Miyazaki to persist to the end, so he will do so.

[]: Post by Chuckman » Sun Dec 21, 2014 12:05 am The Eva in your mind is the true Eva.

[]: Post by EvangelionGodMode » Mon Dec 22, 2014 12:43 am The only depressing part for me is when it ends and i have to go watch something else. Trying to find something on par and as intriguing as Eva is hard.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 22, 2014 2:39 am EvangelionGodMode wrote: The only depressing part for me is when it ends and i have to go watch something else. Trying to find something on par and as intriguing as Eva is hard.

[]: Post by Merkaba » Mon Dec 22, 2014 7:54 am It is depressing. On the one hand, I wish the movies would never end, because there's never been a point in my life where I've ever thought, "wow, I really hope I never get to see new Eva again." On the other hand, every story needs an ending, and I need to see how this tale is wrapped up. It's been an incredible ride and I have zero doubts that Anno is going to deliver something phenomenal. So yeah, happy to see how everything plays out, depressed that this is essentially it for an Anno-driven Eva series.

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Mon Dec 22, 2014 9:43 am Merkaba wrote: So yeah, happy to see how everything plays out, depressed that this is essentially it for an Anno-driven Eva series.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Dec 22, 2014 10:20 am ...Which I quoted a few posts above yours.

[]: Post by Sicarius VI » Mon Dec 22, 2014 12:00 pm ElMariachi wrote: Didn't Anno said recently that he would eventually come back to Eva sometime after NTE?

[]: Post by Dataprime » Mon Dec 22, 2014 12:50 pm Evangelion - the gift that keeps on giving

[]: Post by pwhodges » Mon Dec 22, 2014 1:31 pm Dataprime wrote: Evangelion - the gift that keeps on holding back

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 22, 2014 1:42 pm Sicarius VI wrote: I thought Anno was done with Evangelion after NTE, am I wrong?

[]: Post by Dataprime » Mon Dec 22, 2014 1:59 pm It's not all about the fans. Think about poor Anno and all the stress he is under to make the damn thing.

[]: Post by Sicarius VI » Mon Dec 22, 2014 2:18 pm Reichu wrote: The entitlement is strong with this one.

[]: Post by Sorrow » Mon Dec 22, 2014 2:31 pm There are many other ways one can be productive. More productive than going outside, too. Why not attempt to create something yourself? Draw up a plan?
